fields.description:
## Why
P6 flags a MISSING verify command; it does not catch a PRESENT command that silently lies. A mechanically-checkable subset fits the DET floor (polyglot, fail-open, coverage-recording).

## What (verified constraint)
- DET_CHECKS in det_floor.py is FROZEN P1-P9 per docs/adr/0016-project-det-invariants.md (NOTE: there are two ADR-0016 files; the DET one is 0016-project-det-invariants.md), and tests/unit/test_det_invariants.py hard-asserts it. So do NOT add a P10: EXTEND p6_ac_quality (det_floor.py def@493). Consider shellcheck as prior art before a bespoke regex set.
- Flag three defect classes: (1) pure file-inspection with no assertion (grep/find/ls/wc/cat/head with no test/comparison/exit-code check); (2) unanchored-grep patterns; (3) unquoted shell metacharacters. Language-agnostic + fail-open: a line in an unparseable stack ABSTAINS (recorded in the DetResult coverage dict), never false-accuses. NOTE: p6_ac_quality emits ONE DetResult with a flat coverage dict, so per-line abstains must aggregate into that dict (not per-line events).
- LLM-tier depth of this taxonomy is WS5/E6 (sibling) -- out of scope here.

## Provenance
Gap-report labels (G-*/FP-*/R-*/WS-N) are tags from ~/dso_rebar_plan_review_gap_report.md + session log brief-bride-rash (95f1-1b3b-a867-4020), NOT codebase symbols. Symbols introduced by a SIBLING story (e.g. committed_work_relies_on_unbacked_claim from WS1) are target-state, not pre-existing; the dependency is recorded.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] p6_ac_quality extended with the verify-command lint. Proof: `pytest tests/unit/test_det_invariants.py` green after update (DET_CHECKS still P1-P9).
- [ ] Fixture set covers ALL THREE defect classes (one command each) PLUS one unknown/unparseable line; unit test asserts fire on the three and abstain (coverage-recorded) on the unknown. Proof: `pytest tests/unit/test_det_invariants.py -k verify_command_lint` green.
- [ ] shellcheck prior-art note recorded in the criterion's code comment (adopt or justify bespoke).
